name: Easy Tuscan Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Dried tomatoes in oil, drained
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl combine yeast, salt and 2 cups flour.
2. In a 1-quart saucepan over medium heat, heat sugar, 3 tablespoons olive oil or salad oil, and 1 1/4 cups water until very warm (125-130 degrees).
3. With mixer at low speed, beat liquid into dry ingredients just until blended.
4. Increase speed to medium; beat for 2 minutes.
5. Add 1/2 cup flour, beat for 2 more minutes.
6. With a spoon, stir in the remaining 1 1/2 cups flour to make soft dough.
7. Turn dough onto a floured surface and knead until smooth and elastic, about 8-10 minutes, working in more flour (about 1/2 cup) while kneading.
8. Shape dough into a ball; cover and let rest for 15 minutes.
9. Grease and flour a jelly roll pan; pat dough into the pan, pushing it well into corners.
10. Cover, let rise in a warm place (80-85 degrees) until doubled, about 30 minutes.
11. While dough is rising, in a small saucepan over medium heat, cook sliced garlic in 2 tablespoons olive or salad oil, stirring frequently until garlic is golden.
12. With a slotted spoon, remove and discard garlic.
13. Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
14. With fingers, make deep indentations over the entire surface of dough.
15. Drizzle garlic oil over dough; top with grated Parmesan cheese, dried rosemary, coarse salt, and coarse ground black pepper.
16. Bake for 10 minutes.
17. Meanwhile, slice each dried tomato lengthwise into half.
18. Remove focaccia from oven; top with dried tomatoes and garnish with fresh rosemary sprigs.
19. Bake for an additional 5-10 minutes until bread is golden brown.
